Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 537 upvotes, 76 comments
  2. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 114 upvotes, 13 comments
  3. Caxlsx: Ruby gem for xlsx generation with charts, images, schema validation 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Show HN: Remotely use my guitar tuner 232 upvotes, 50 comments
  5. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 43 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. FreeBSD 14.4-Release Announcement 90 upvotes, 22 comments
  7. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 351 upvotes, 111 comments
  8. LoGeR – 3D reconstruction from extremely long videos (DeepMind, UC Berkeley) 94 upvotes, 24 comments
  9. Two Years of Emacs Solo 302 upvotes, 102 comments
  10. Darkrealms BBS 120 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 155 upvotes, 49 comments
  12. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 143 upvotes, 53 comments
  14. I put my whole life into a single database 228 upvotes, 99 comments
  15. Traffic from Russia to Cloudflare is 60% down from last year 29 upvotes, 8 comments
  16. TCXO Failure Analysis 62 upvotes, 23 comments
  17. Sending Jabber/XMPP Messages via HTTP 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. Practical Guide to Bare Metal C++ 50 upvotes, 20 comments
  19. The hidden compile-time cost of C++26 reflection 48 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 83 upvotes, 41 comments
  21. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 209 upvotes, 124 comments
  22. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 339 upvotes, 244 comments
  23. SHOW HN: A usage circuit breaker for Cloudflare Workers 11 upvotes, 4 comments
  24. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 523 upvotes, 522 comments
  25. Rebasing in Magit 37 upvotes, 26 comments
  26. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 252 upvotes, 256 comments
  27. Show HN: DD Photos – open-source photo album site generator (Go and SvelteKit) 9 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. Germany's Solar Boom Eases Power Costs as Gas Price Jumps 38 upvotes, 33 comments
  29. Hisense TVs add unskippable startup ads before live TV 56 upvotes, 65 comments
  30. EVi, a Hard-Fork of Vim 31 upvotes, 36 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 10 March 2026. Source here.
